notes: Test framework deviation: plan called for bats; using pytest+subprocess instead (bats not installed; pytest already in dev deps). Test files live at tests/test_*.py per project convention rather than tests/scripts/*.bats.

tasks.title: Storage abstraction helper (scripts/lib/_artifact_store.sh)
acceptance_criteria: scripts/lib/_artifact_store.sh defines: _put_artifact <local_path> <artifact_name>, _get_artifact <artifact_name> <local_path>, _list_artifacts (prints names), _stat_artifact <artifact_name> (prints 'bytes mtime_epoch' on one line), _artifact_exists <artifact_name>

Dispatch is keyed on STORAGE_BACKEND env var (default 'nextcloud'); only 'nextcloud' implemented today, but the case statement has a default branch that errors with 'unknown STORAGE_BACKEND: $X — supported: nextcloud' so future backends slot in cleanly
Nextcloud backend writes under ${NEXTCLOUD_DATASETS:-$HOME/Nextcloud/Datasets}/db_sync/ and uses a .partial suffix during write, mv-ing into place only on success (atomic finalization)
All functions return 0 on success / non-zero on failure with a stderr message; no silent failures
File is sourceable (set -u safe) and has no side effects when sourced — does not create the db_sync/ directory until first _put_artifact call
tests/scripts/test_artifact_store.bats covers: round-trip, .partial cleanup on failed write, _stat_artifact format, unknown backend error
Full test suite still green

tasks.title: Shared manifest helper (scripts/lib/_manifest.sh)
tasks.test_file: tests/test_manifest.py (18 tests passing, 1 skipped — jq is installed so the missing-jq path can't be triggered)
acceptance_criteria: scripts/lib/_manifest.sh defines: _manifest_init <out_file> (writes schema_version, exported_at, source_host, git_commit), _manifest_add_db <out_file> <db_key> <archive_name> <bytes> <sha256> <db_name> <counts_json>, _manifest_finalize <out_file> (atomically mv from .partial), _manifest_read <in_file>, _manifest_verify_archive <manifest_file> <db_key> <local_archive_path> (checks sha256, returns non-zero on mismatch)

Manifest schema matches the JSON shape documented in exploration.md §6 (schema_version=1, exported_at ISO-8601 UTC, source_host=$(hostname), git_commit=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD || echo 'unknown'), per-DB block with archive/bytes/sha256/db_name/key_counts)
Uses jq for read/write — script errors with a clear message if jq is missing (require_cmd jq); no Python dependency
sha256 computation uses sha256sum (Linux); manifest stores hex string
tests/scripts/test_manifest.bats covers: round-trip read/write, corrupted manifest rejection, sha256 mismatch rejection, missing jq error
Full test suite still green

tasks.title: scripts/sync_pg.sh — export + import subcommands
acceptance_criteria: scripts/sync_pg.sh mirrors sync_mongo.sh structure: same shebang, same colour helpers (ok/warn/err), same env-file loading from ~/.myenvs/ema_nlp.env, same subcommand dispatch pattern

Subcommand 'export': runs `docker exec ema_nlp_pg pg_dump --format=custom --compress=6 --no-owner --no-privileges -U $POSTGRES_USER -d $POSTGRES_DB` and pipes stdout to a local temp file, then calls _put_artifact temp pg.dump; writes a per-DB manifest fragment via _manifest_add_db

Subcommand 'import': calls _get_artifact pg.dump to a temp path, runs `docker exec -i ema_nlp_pg pg_restore --clean --if-exists --no-owner --no-privileges -U $POSTGRES_USER -d $POSTGRES_DB` < tempfile after a confirm-before-drop prompt (unless --yes is set)
Both subcommands accept and honour --yes (suppresses interactive confirmation, still prints what is about to happen)
Health-check before export: errors with a clear message if `docker ps --filter name=ema_nlp_pg --filter status=running` is empty; tells the user to run scripts/start_services.sh
Health-check before import: same container check, plus verifies the target DB exists (`SELECT 1`) and applies scripts/init_db.py if not — restore needs the DB present even with --clean
Reports `documents`, `chunks`, `links` row counts on both export and import (via `docker exec ema_nlp_pg psql -tAc 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ...'`)
--help / -h prints usage matching sync_mongo.sh style
tests/scripts/test_sync_pg.bats covers: --help works, export errors when container down, --yes flag suppresses prompt; round-trip integration test is deferred to DBSYNC-010
Full test suite still green

tasks.title: Extend scripts/sync_mongo.sh — gzip, sha256, --yes, consolidated path
acceptance_criteria: sync_mongo.sh now writes to ~/Nextcloud/Datasets/db_sync/mongo.archive.gz via _put_artifact (was: ~/Nextcloud/Datasets/mongo_sync/ema_scraper.archive uncompressed)
Adds --gzip to mongodump and mongorestore invocations; archive is ~60% smaller
Computes sha256 on export and writes a per-DB manifest fragment via _manifest_add_db; import calls _manifest_verify_archive before touching the live DB
--yes flag added to export, import, and pull subcommands (suppresses confirm-before-drop)

One-time migration on first export: if the legacy ~/Nextcloud/Datasets/mongo_sync/ema_scraper.archive exists, decompress + recompress it into the new path and leave a symlink at the old path; prints a deprecation warning pointing at docs/SYNC.md. Idempotent.
Backward compat: running sync_mongo.sh standalone (no wrapper) still works end-to-end, including the existing pull subcommand
tests/scripts/test_sync_mongo_extensions.bats covers: --yes suppresses prompt, gzip archive readable by mongorestore --gzip, migration creates the symlink, sha256 mismatch is rejected on import
scripts/sync_mongo.sh header comment updated to reflect the new path + gzip + sha256
Full test suite still green

tasks.title: --no-embeddings flag on sync_pg.sh
acceptance_criteria: sync_pg.sh export accepts --no-embeddings; passes --exclude-table-data=chunks to pg_dump (schema kept; data dropped); also passes --exclude-table-data=chunks for the related indexes to keep dump small
The flag is recorded in the per-DB manifest as `embeddings_excluded: true` (default: false)
sync_pg.sh import detects `embeddings_excluded: true` from the manifest and prints a post-restore hint: 'chunks table is empty — run `python -m harness.embed_pg` on this machine to repopulate'
Archive size assertion in test: --no-embeddings dump of a 5k-chunk DB is < 10% of the full dump size

tests/scripts/test_sync_pg_no_embeddings.bats covers the flag end-to-end against a fixture-loaded test PG (uses ema_nlp_test database, follows the pattern from tests/test_embed_pg_sync_with_link_graph.py)
Full test suite still green

tasks.title: scripts/sync_databases.sh wrapper — export, import, status
acceptance_criteria: scripts/sync_databases.sh accepts subcommands: export, import, status, --help

export: calls `bash scripts/sync_mongo.sh export --yes` then `bash scripts/sync_pg.sh export --yes`; on success of both, writes the combined manifest.json LAST via _manifest_finalize; rolls back (deletes both archives) if either per-DB export fails so a partial state is never visible to the receiver

import: calls _manifest_read first; refuses if manifest.json absent; verifies both archives via _manifest_verify_archive; prints source_host / exported_at / archive sizes / key_counts comparison vs. local; single combined [y/N] prompt; then `bash scripts/sync_mongo.sh import --yes --skip-checksum` + `bash scripts/sync_pg.sh import --yes --skip-checksum` (per-DB scripts skip their own sha256 check since the wrapper already verified)
status: read-only; shows archive presence/age/size/source-host vs. local DB counts; exits 0 if archives present and 1 if missing — so the command can be used in shell pipelines
--only mongo|pg flag: dispatches to only one per-DB script; manifest still written/read but with only the relevant block populated
--no-embeddings flag: passed through to sync_pg.sh export only
tests/scripts/test_sync_databases.bats covers: --help, status against empty Nextcloud dir, status against populated archive, --only flag dispatches correctly
Full test suite still green

tasks.title: sync_pg.sh pull subcommand (SSH live mode)
acceptance_criteria: sync_pg.sh pull --host X [--user Y] [--port Z] mirrors sync_mongo.sh pull: parses flags, defaults from PG_SYNC_HOST / PG_SYNC_USER / PG_SYNC_SSH_PORT env vars

Pipeline: `ssh -p $PORT $USER@$HOST 'docker exec ema_nlp_pg pg_dump --format=custom -Z 6 --no-owner --no-privileges -U $POSTGRES_USER -d $POSTGRES_DB' | docker exec -i ema_nlp_pg pg_restore --clean --if-exists --no-owner --no-privileges -U $POSTGRES_USER -d $POSTGRES_DB`
Pre-flight: ssh into remote, run `docker ps --filter name=ema_nlp_pg --filter status=running`; error if remote container is down
Reports remote chunks count and local chunks count before + after (smoke check parity)
Honours --yes flag
tests/scripts/test_sync_pg_pull.bats covers flag parsing only (network paths are skipped unless PG_SYNC_HOST is set, matching sync_mongo.sh pull's testing pattern)
Full test suite still green

tasks.title: Conflict warning on import (stale-overwrite guard)
acceptance_criteria: sync_databases.sh import compares the archive's manifest.exported_at against the local DBs' most-recent-write timestamps before the destructive prompt: PG = MAX(documents.ingested_at), Mongo = MAX(web_items.ingested_at) (both via `docker exec` / `mongosh --quiet --eval`)

If local last-write > archive.exported_at, prints a yellow warning block: '[!] Local DB has writes newer than the incoming archive (local: TS, archive: TS). Importing will DESTROY them.' followed by an extra confirm prompt that requires typing the word 'overwrite' (not just Y)
--force flag skips the extra confirm (still prints the warning)
Falls back gracefully when local DB is empty (no comparison made; prints '[ok] local DB is empty — clean import')
tests/scripts/test_conflict_guard.bats covers: archive newer than local (no warning), local newer than archive (warning + extra confirm required), --force skips the extra confirm
Full test suite still green

tasks.title: docs/SYNC.md — operator's guide + CLAUDE.md/SETUP.md cross-links
acceptance_criteria: docs/SYNC.md exists with sections: §1 Round-trip recipe (export → wait for Nextcloud → import), §2 New-machine bootstrap (clone → start_services.sh → sync_databases.sh import), §3 Storage layout (db_sync/ contents + manifest schema), §4 Conflict semantics (last-writer-wins + the stale-overwrite guard), §5 Troubleshooting (sha256 mismatch, partial Nextcloud upload, HNSW rebuild time, container not running)
Includes concrete time + size estimates from exploration.md §5
docs/SETUP.md gains a §8 'Cross-machine sync' subsection that points at docs/SYNC.md
CLAUDE.md 'Data sources' section gains a one-line pointer to docs/SYNC.md for the cross-machine workflow
deploy/mongo/README.md gains a note that the cross-machine workflow uses mongodump-style logical dumps, not bind-mount sharing
.claude/HISTORY.md gets an entry summarising the work unit (one row, the standard format)

tasks.title: End-to-end smoke test on this laptop
acceptance_criteria: On the laptop with the native Mongo 7.0.22 running and Postgres started via deploy/postgres/docker-compose.yml: bash scripts/sync_databases.sh status shows 0 local PG docs + 115k mongo web_items + 'no archive on Nextcloud yet'
Run sync_databases.sh export end-to-end; verify db_sync/{mongo.archive.gz, pg.dump, manifest.json} appear; manifest.source_host == 'schleppi'; sha256s match files on disk
Re-run sync_databases.sh import on the same machine; round-trip succeeds; local counts unchanged after import (within tolerance for non-deterministic timestamps)
Deliberately truncate mongo.archive.gz by 100 bytes; re-run import; verify the script aborts BEFORE the DROP prompt with a sha256 mismatch error
Restore the archive; run sync_databases.sh export --no-embeddings; verify pg.dump is < 50 MB (with current empty PG, basically schema-only)
Document the run in a new HISTORY.md entry (date 2026-05-30 or later); update state.json status to 'completed'; mark work unit done
